svg.text: TAMIL
DRAVIDIAN PHONOLOGY
TAMIL CONSONANT FEATURES
No aspiration contrast (unlike Sanskrit, Telugu, Hindi)
Five places of articulation with full retroflexes:
Velar k • Palatal c • Retroflex T • Dental t • Labial p
zh /ʒ/ retroflex approximant: ONLY in Tamil and Malayalam
Distinguishes: l /l/ • L /ɭ/ (retroflex lateral) • zh /ʒ/
CONTEXT-DEPENDENT ALLOPHONES
Tamil consonants change sound by position in word:
k = /k/ at start of word • /ɡ/ between vowels
t = /t/ at start • /ð/ or /d/ between vowels
p = /p/ initially • /b/ or /β/ medially
TAMIL VOWELS
5 short + 5 long: a/ā i/ī u/ū e/ē o/ō + ai au
Length is phonemic: paal "milk" • pal "tooth"
No nasal vowels (unlike Sanskrit)
TELUGU PHONOLOGY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
TELUGU CONSONANT SYSTEM
Four-way stop contrast (more than Tamil):
voiceless plain / voiceless aspirate / voiced plain / voiced aspirate
p ph b bh / t th d dh / k kh g gh
Borrowed from Sanskrit; Telugu integrated full Sanskrit phonology
TELUGU VOWELS
Similar 5+5 short/long vowel system: a/ā i/ī u/ū e/ē o/ō
No vowel reduction; all vowels pronounced fully
Telugu: no zh /ʒ/ (that retroflex approximant is Tamil-specific)
DRAVIDIAN PHONOLOGICAL PARALLELS
Both: retroflex series T D N L (Dravidian hallmark)
Both: 5+5 short/long vowels • both: no tone
Both: SOV word order • both: agglutinative morphology
Tamil: no aspiration, zh present • Telugu: aspiration, no zh
Telugu more Sanskrit-influenced phonologically than Tamil
PLATE 14b ALT · TAMIL / TELUGU · PHONOLOGY
